excluded_categories.reason: No permissions-related requirements are defined in the summary.
No explicit dependencies or integration points (e.g., email service) are specified; internal components are not defined.
No network conditions (e.g., latency, offline) are mentioned.

scenario_generation_rules: Generate positive test cases using valid data for all required fields: first name, last name, username, phone number (with plausible digits), email (generic format like user@example.com), password meeting complexity (8-12 chars, includes at least one number, one alphabet, one special character), and matching confirm password.

Generate negative test cases for each validation rule: empty required fields, password too short (<8) or too long (>12), missing number, missing special character, missing alphabet, confirm password mismatch, duplicate email address.
Generate boundary test cases for password length: minimum 8 characters, maximum 12 characters, one less than min (7), one more than max (13).

Generate business rule test cases: (1) Attempt login with unverified email – should fail with meaningful error. (2) After email verification, login should succeed. (3) Register with an already used email – should be rejected with duplicate email error.
Generate security test cases: Ensure password complexity is strictly enforced; confirm that unverified accounts cannot log in.

Generate UX/UI test cases: Verify that inline error messages appear for each invalid field, messages are descriptive (e.g., 'Password must be 8-12 characters'), and messages clear when input is corrected.

Exclude any test cases that rely on undefined specifications: email format regex, allowed special characters in password, phone number format, username uniqueness, email verification process details (expiry, link format), HTTP status codes, and error message examples. These are listed as open questions.
For tests involving special characters in password, use a common special character like '!' since the allowed set is undefined, but do not assume a specific set.
